channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jude Law and Nicholas Hoult lead thriller about the real FBI manhunt for a white supremacist leader
item.description: Forty years ago, on Dec. 8, 1984, one of the largest manhunts in FBI history came to an explosive end on Whidbey Island in Puget Sound. The person they were after was Robert Jay Mathews, the leader of a white supremacist group committing armed robberies to finance plans to overthrow the government.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Great Lakes region braces for more snow while cleaning up after lake-effect storms
item.description: Even more snow could be on the way for places in the Great Lakes region that are still digging out after days of storms caused deadly wrecks, collapsed a barn on top of 100 cows and buried some towns under nearly 6 feet (1.8 meters).
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: We're all saying Daniel Craig's name wrong
item.description: His name may no longer be "Bond, James Bond," but former 007 Daniel Craig is setting the record straight about how to pronounce his name.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KRON4

item.title: Missouri man facing execution in the sexual assault and strangling of a 9-year-old girl
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — A Missouri man is scheduled to be put to death Tuesday evening for sexually assaulting and strangling a 9-year-old girl whose body was thrown into a sinkhole. Christopher Collings, 49, was set to receive a single injection of the sedative pentobarbital at or after 6 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Babies found safe after car stolen and infants dumped in cold, IMPD says
item.description: Shortly before noon, officers located the stolen vehicle. The babies were not inside and the suspect was gone.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: What happened to Hannah Kobayashi? Police say the Hawaii woman voluntarily disappeared
item.description: Police say a 30-year-old woman from Hawaii who was reported missing in Los Angeles three weeks ago voluntarily traveled to Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Transgender powerlifter asks Minnesota Supreme Court to let her compete in women's events
item.description: Attorneys for a transgender athlete have urged the Minnesota Supreme Court to allow her to compete in the women's division at powerlifting events, saying she’s protected against discrimination by the Minnesota Human Rights Act.
item.dc:creator: Steve Karnowski, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Daniel Penny jurors begin deliberations in Jordan Neely subway chokehold trial
item.description: Jurors have begun their deliberations in the trial of Daniel Penny, who is charged with manslaughter and criminally negligent homicide for Jordan Neely's death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jury deliberations begin in veteran Daniel Penny's trial over using chokehold on Jordan Neely
item.description: Jurors have started deliberating in the trial of a military veteran charged with using a fatal chokehold to subdue a man whose behavior was alarming passengers on a New York subway train.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FBI offers $15K reward in case of Maine teen missing since September
item.description: The teenager reportedly had an argument with her sister before walking out of her house into the woods.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KREX 5

item.title: California teacher dies after suspected bat bite in her classroom
item.description: A central California middle school teacher died after contracting rabies from a suspected bat bite, local health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illegal immigrant from China shipped weapons to North Korea from California: DOJ
item.description: An illegal immigrant from China allegedly shipped weapons from California to North Korea, according to federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family wants answers after FedEx truck hits dog
item.description: ELIZABETHTON, Tenn. (WJHL) - The Burleson family's pet dog Bear was injured after they say a FedEx driver delivered a package and then drove off, hitting the dog. Now, they're seeking answers and wanting an apology directly from the driver. Jessica Burleson adopted Bear 14 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jayonna Scurry, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Wisconsin's collective bargaining law for public workers
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A judge's overturning of Wisconsin's 13-year-old law that effectively ended collective bargaining for teachers and most state government employees has rekindled a battle over labor rights in a state where the first public sector unions were formed 65 years ago.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The drinking water for New Jersey's capital went largely untested for more than a year, utility says
item.description: A utility says the drinking water in New Jersey’s capital city and some surrounding communities went largely untested for more than a year, and that it fired a worker who falsified the relevant reports.
item.dc:creator: Mike Catalini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he would block US Steel acquisition by Nippon Steel
item.description: The Japanese firm announced last year that it would acquire U.S. Steel Corp. in a $14.9 billion deal — a move that was sharply criticized by lawmakers on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pressley: Biden 'must act' on clemency before leaving office
item.description: Rep. Ayanna Pressley (D-Mass.) is renewing calls for President Biden to grant clemency to thousands of Americans serving time in federal institutions following his bombshell pardon of his son, Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez first person to hit a million followers on Bluesky
item.description: The platform’s features are based off of Twitter, and they include the ability to message fellow users as well as a discover and feed tab.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Former ballerina, Ashley Benefield, sentenced to decades in prison
item.description: Ashley Benefield, who was found guilty of manslaughter for shooting her husband during a domestic argument in 2020 sentencing hearing is set for today.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, NewsNation

item.title: GM recalls over 130,000 trucks over unexpected tailgate unlatching
item.description: Over 132,000 Chevrolet and GMC trucks are facing a recall over concerns that the tailgate may unlatch unexpectedly.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police say a Hawaii woman disappeared voluntarily and traveled to Mexico
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Hawaii woman who vanished after landing in Los Angeles three weeks ago disappeared voluntarily as she sought to “step away from modern connectivity” and was last seen crossing into Mexico with her luggage, police said at a news conference where they urged her to contact her dist
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Here's the top 25 list of most-viewed Wikipedia articles of 2024
item.description: We collectively spent an estimated 2.4 billion hours reading English-language articles on Wikipedia in 2024, and we now know which were the most popular.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, ABC 4

item.title: Hunter Biden pardon will undermine party's 'self-proclaimed authority' on rule of law: Democratic strategist
item.description: Democratic strategist Julian Epstein says President Biden's decision to pardon his son, Hunter, undermines "Democrats' self-proclaimed authority on the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Biden faces backlash over pardon of Hunter and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son Hunter threatens to tarnish legacy
item.description: President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter in his final full month in the White House is threatening to erode his legacy as he prepares to leave office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Brown University transfers ownership of a portion of its land to Pokanoket Indian Tribe
item.description: Brown University has transferred ownership of a portion of its land in Bristol, Rhode Island, to a preservation trust established by the Pokanoket Indian Tribe.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New concrete formula could be key to fixing the roads: Michigan researchers
item.description: A team of researchers at Michigan State University claim this new type of concrete is “flexible, self-heating and self-healing.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, ABC 4

item.title: Lake-effect snow blankets the Great Lakes as Thanksgiving travelers head home
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Lake-effect snow blanketed swaths of the U.S. as Thanksgiving travelers journeyed home this holiday weekend, with more snow expected to fall over the Great Lakes region through the week.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, CAROLYN THOMPSON and JONAH BRONSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Coast Guard is searching for 5 people after a fishing boat reportedly capsized in seas off Alaska
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard said Sunday it's searching for five people after a fishing boat reportedly capsized in rough weather and seas near Alaska's capital of Juneau.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mitchell scores 35, Cavs end Celtics' winning streak with 115-111 win in matchup of East's elite
item.description: Donovan Mitchell scored 20 of his 35 points in the fourth quarter as the Cleveland Cavaliers ended Boston's seven-game winning streak with a 115-111 victory over the Celtics on Sunday night in the second intense matchup between the Eastern Conference’s top teams this season.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in Death Valley National Park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, WGN 9
